## Country launch order

Countries are listed cheapest-cost-per-respondent first, matching the round-robin priority
used in the simulation. Recruitment is dynamic: each participant may see a different set of
15 statements, adjusted after every completed session (see `strategy.md`, Step 6). There is no
longer a fixed "block of 10" — the participant counts below are individuals.

---

## Notes

- **Re-run before each batch launch.** `answers.csv` is refreshed nightly. Re-running
  `python simulate_budget.py` recomputes `n(i,j)`, `R(i)`, and the launch plan from scratch.
- **`statement_selection.csv`** lists every (country, participant, slot, statement) row in
  machine-readable form.
- **No fixed blocks.** Unlike the 2026-06-29 strategy, participants are not grouped into
  identical-statement batches of 10 — each participant's 15 statements come from that
  country's live active set at the moment they are recruited.
- **Concurrency/timeouts not modeled here.** This simulation assumes every recruited
  participant completes their session. The live system must track `pending(i,j)` reservations
  with a 45-minute timeout so concurrent sessions don't over-assign the same near-complete
  cell — see `strategy.md`, Step 7.
